mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declines in industrial production indicate supply-side contraction that outweighs rate hike benefits, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the signal direction flips to positive.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.01 over any 36-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market expansion phases, surges in housing starts signal immediate construction sector revenue growth and broader economic optimism, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during housing expansions produce negative 5-day forward returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the signal loses predictive power outside the specified regime.
